input_candidates.truth_value: Density reduction by Al: approximately 1.3% per 1 wt.% Al addition (from literature); not directly measured for these alloys.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Martensite start temperatures (Ms): S1 277 °C, S2 274 °C, S3 258 °C, S4 305 °C (1150 °C) / 310 °C (1250 °C), S5 331 °C.

input_candidates.truth_value: Table 2 compiles MatCalc-predicted, measured, and applied TA temperatures and Ms values for each steel.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Product of strength and elongation (PSE) values: S1 at 300 °C 10,880 MPa·% and at 350 °C ~12,000 MPa·%; S2 at 300 °C 11,130 MPa·% and at 350 °C 12,970 MPa·%; S3 at 350 °C 11,150 MPa·% and at 270 °C ~3,480 MPa·%; S4 at 1250/350 °C 11,700 MPa·%, at 1150/320 °C 13,380 MPa·%, and at 1150/350 °C 13,600 MPa·%; S5 at 335 °C 10,710 MPa·% and at 365 °C 11,450 MPa·%.

input_candidates.truth_value: For S3 at TB 270 °C, bainitic transformation required 43,200 s; an intermediate microstructure at 25,200 s still showed incomplete transformation.
input_candidates.prediction_value: HV30 hardness values across all studied alloys ranged between 450 and 500 HV30, generally higher at lower austempering temperatures except for S4.

input_candidates.truth_value: LOM images at 1050 °C benchmark show αp retention in S1, S3, S4, S5; S2 is αp-free at this temperature.
input_candidates.prediction_value: MatCalc-predicted ferrite-free austenitizing temperatures: S1 991 °C, S2 929 °C, S3 1177 °C, S4 1016 °C, S5 986 °C; measured operational TA: S1 1062 °C, S2 1031 °C, S3 broadened transition, S4 1067 °C, S5 ~900 °C.

TTP simulations indicated that (Fe,M)xCy precipitation during cooling is unavoidable in S1 and S3 between approximately 700 °C and 1150 °C, and in S5 between 700 °C and 1000 °C; in S4, initial (Fe,M)xCy nucleation is suppressed by the counter-coarsening effect of the Ti–Mo–C precipitate system.

input_candidates.truth_value: Figure captions indicate extensive microscopy (Fig. 9,10,11) and simulation results (Fig. 4,8) that could not be fully captured due to lack of image pixel data; trends and labeled features are included where explicitly stated in captions or text.
input_candidates.prediction_value: For S3, a slow bainite growth period following incubation lasted nearly 3000 s at 270 °C and 1500 s at 350 °C, attributed to carbon enrichment around pre-existing polygonal ferrite stabilizing adjacent austenite and increasing the energy barrier for transformation.

candidates.truth_value: Density reduction by Al: approximately 1.3% per 1 wt.% Al addition (from literature); not directly measured for these alloys.
candidates.prediction_value: Martensite start temperatures (Ms): S1 277 °C, S2 274 °C, S3 258 °C, S4 305 °C (1150 °C) / 310 °C (1250 °C), S5 331 °C.
candidates.truth_normalized: density reduction by al: approximately 1.3% per 1 wt.% al addition (from literature); not directly measured for these alloys.
candidates.prediction_normalized: martensite start temperatures (ms): s1 277 c , s2 274 c , s3 258 c , s4 305 c (1150 c ) / 310 c (1250 c ), s5 331 c .
